whats_new.headline: Unitree releases open-source world model for humanoid teleoperation
whats_new.detail: New benchmark shows 1.4x improvement in sim-to-real transfer on contact-rich manipulation tasks using a causal world model as the planning substrate.

whats_new.headline: EU AI Act compliance deadline triggers enterprise spend on attestation rails
whats_new.detail: Forrester pegs 2026 enterprise spend on compliance-attestation middleware at $4.2B, up 38% YoY. W3C Verifiable Credentials named as the fastest-growing substrate.

whats_new.headline: Solana DePIN programs cross $1B TVL — institutional flows dominate
whats_new.detail: On-chain data shows 63% of new DePIN-program deposits in Q1 2026 came from wallets tagged as institutional. DDIL-tolerant compute markets lead the category.

ten_x_improvement.title: Pipe Civium's EPCIS 2.0 attestations into Aureon's FitIQ as a positive-signal feature
ten_x_improvement.summary: Civium already emits W3C VC 2.0 compliance attestations on every supplier state change. Today those attestations only flow as negative signals (VIOLATION → FitIQ penalty). Flipping the sign so CLEAN attestations count as positive FitIQ evidence would turn every compliance event into a dual-purpose procurement signal — without any new data collection.
implementation_steps: Extend the COMPLIANCE_STATE_CHANGE causal rule to emit a POSITIVE_ATTESTATION effect when status === 'COMPLIANT' and score >= 85.
Add Aureon ingest handler APPLY_POSITIVE_ATTESTATION that bumps FitIQ by a decayed factor of the attestation score.
Backfill the last 90 days of Civium attestations to seed the positive-signal FitIQ adjustment.
Measure precision/recall lift on APP-Bench NDCG@20 vs. the control cohort.

thirty_day_plan.week_1: Add positive-attestation branch to causal-rules.ts and wire Aureon ingest handler.
thirty_day_plan.week_2: Backfill job + offline APP-Bench evaluation on the expanded feature set.
thirty_day_plan.week_3: Shadow-mode deploy: log positive-attestation FitIQ adjustments without applying them.
thirty_day_plan.week_4: Ramp to 10%/50%/100% of suppliers. Monitor FitIQ distribution drift and NDCG@20.

ten_x_improvement.financial_impact: Projected 8–12% lift in supplier-match acceptance rates on the enterprise pilot cohort. At current ARR of $3.1M per enterprise seat, a 10% acceptance lift translates to ~$310K ACV per seat.

research_backing.title: EPCIS 2.0 dual-signal modeling (GS1 2025 whitepaper)
research_backing.detail: GS1 formalizes positive-attestation semantics in EPCIS 2.0. Adopters report 15–22% gains in supply-chain trust scoring when compliance events are modeled as signed deltas rather than binary gates.

research_backing.title: IBM Research — Attestation-weighted procurement ranking
research_backing.detail: Internal experiment at IBM shows a 9.4% NDCG@20 improvement when verifiable credentials are blended into supplier ranking as continuous positive features.

successful.what_they_did: Integrated EPCIS 2.0 clean attestations into their procurement ML ranker in late 2025.
successful.outcome: Reported 11% reduction in supplier-qualification cycle time and 7% lift in on-time delivery for the top-scored tier.

successful.what_they_did: Used positive compliance signals from their internal attestation registry to re-weight the supplier graph for capital-equipment sourcing.
successful.outcome: Closed a $180M wind-turbine contract 3 weeks earlier than the prior baseline cycle — attributed in part to faster supplier verification.

failed.what_they_did: Tried to use raw on-chain attestations as a procurement signal without decaying the score or modeling provenance.
failed.outcome: Ranker overfit to a small cluster of highly-attested suppliers; supplier diversity dropped 24% in 6 months, triggering an ESG compliance incident.

failed.what_they_did: Piloted a compliance-only sourcing rule that boosted suppliers with perfect attestation records.
failed.outcome: Cost per load rose 14% because the highest-attested suppliers were also the most expensive. Pilot ended after one quarter.
